title: Oldenlandia thwaitesii
intro: Oldenlandia thwaitesii, also known as Sri Lankan creeping milkwort, is a small, creeping herb belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia thwaitesii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Oldenlandia thwaitesii is commonly used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and powdered extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive disorders, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia thwaitesii belongs to the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the acanthus or thistle family. This family consists of flowering plants with more than 2,000 species. The genus Oldenlandia is part of the tribe Ruaceae and is characterized by its herbaceous nature and small flowers. Oldenlandia thwaitesii is a species of flowering plant native to Sri Lanka. It is a relatively understudied species, but its classification is recognized within the Acanthaceae family.

It is said to be effective in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
